title: Aegis AI Governance & Readiness Platform
description: Governance and audit-readiness workstream for AI adoption: readiness scorecards, risk registers, human-review gates, monitoring logic, and executive-facing assessment flows for compliance-sensitive teams.
result: Created a repeatable decision layer that turns vague AI-adoption interest into scored readiness gaps, named risks, review gates, evidence requirements, and pilot decisions.
why: Helps regulated teams decide where AI is safe and worthwhile before procurement or sensitive-data exposure.
stack: AI readiness scoring, risk registers, human-in-the-loop controls, audit evidence, Cloudflare
privacy: Product and deployment shared; private implementation details withheld.

title: PolyRAG: Multi-Model Governance RAG
problem: Policy-heavy teams need grounded answers they can compare against the same retrieved evidence instead of trusting unsupported model fluency.
contribution: Designed and built the ingestion, shared-retrieval, multi-model comparison, citation, evaluation, API, and deployment workflow.
description: Citation-grounded RAG over real AI-governance documents with one retrieval pass and side-by-side answers from multiple OpenAI-compatible models; includes ingestion, FAISS indexing, OCR fallback, evaluation, FastAPI, Docker, and hosted demo.
result: Delivered a shared-retrieval comparison workflow so model answers can be evaluated against the same evidence, citations, and governance corpus instead of incomparable prompts or contexts.
why: Makes LLM comparison more auditable and useful for policy-heavy questions where unsupported fluency is a real risk.
stack: Python, FAISS, FastAPI, Docker, OCR, LLM APIs

title: TransferSignal
description: Private product build for a source-weighted football transfer-rumour ledger: deterministic scoring, source reliability tracking, AI-assisted claim extraction, protected admin workflows, and an editorial newsroom-style board.
result: Unified competing destinations, source grades, supersession, confidence, and editorial context into one traceable transfer ledger rather than a feed of disconnected rumours.
why: Turns noisy sports reporting into an explainable evidence product where readers can inspect why a claim is trusted.
stack: TypeScript, React, PostgreSQL, deterministic scoring, AI extraction, source-reliability analytics
privacy: No private repo or internal code linked; public deployment shared.

title: Stock Analyzer
description: Built a live investment-tracking and market-analysis workspace that brings portfolio monitoring, stock research, and explainable signals into one investor-readable interface without presenting forecasts as certainty.
result: Delivered a working research surface that consolidates investment tracking and analysis into a decision-support workflow instead of a collection of disconnected market screens.
why: Reduces the friction between monitoring a position, investigating a signal, and deciding what deserves deeper research.
stack: Market data, portfolio analytics, time-series signals, financial visualization, explainable decision support
privacy: Live product shared; no brokerage credentials, private holdings, or user financial data are exposed through the portfolio.

title: Multi-Agent AI-Native ERP for Compliance Mapping
description: Active private build for an AI-native operating layer that maps company workflows, obligations, controls, owners, evidence, and readiness gaps across compliance-heavy functions. The focus is company-running ability: turning policies and process knowledge into monitorable execution.
result: Designed one operating model that connects obligations to workflows, controls, evidence, owners, gaps, and recurring execution instead of leaving compliance knowledge across disconnected documents.
why: Makes compliance operational: teams can see what must happen, who owns it, and what evidence proves it happened.
stack: Multi-agent workflows, compliance knowledge graphs, evidence mapping, orchestration, audit trails
privacy: Concept and capability shared; implementation details, schemas, and private code withheld.

title: AI Survey & Interviewing Tool
description: Active product workstream for adaptive AI interviewing, survey design, response synthesis, and evidence extraction. Built around better stakeholder research, readiness assessment, and qualitative-to-quantitative analysis for teams that need structured insight from messy conversations.
role: AI Product Builder / Research Systems Designer
result: Converted open-ended stakeholder conversations into structured evidence, quality-scored responses, comparable themes, and follow-up signals for readiness and discovery work.
why: Preserves qualitative nuance while making stakeholder research usable for prioritization and decision-making.
stack: Adaptive interviewing, structured extraction, multilingual capture, response quality scoring, insight synthesis
privacy: Public description only; no private respondent data, prompts, or implementation details exposed.

title: AI Football Intelligence & Tracking System
description: Active football intelligence workstream spanning transfer signals, source reliability, player/team tracking, tactical context, and editorial-grade dashboards. TransferSignal is one product surface inside the broader sports intelligence direction.
result: Connected source reliability, player and team tracking, tactical context, and editorial presentation into a shared intelligence direction with TransferSignal as a live product surface.
why: Football data becomes more useful when events, sources, roles, and tactical context are evaluated together rather than as isolated metrics.
stack: Sports data pipelines, confidence scoring, source ledgers, tactical analytics, editorial dashboards
privacy: No private repo linked; public-facing deployment links can be added when each surface is ready.

title: IPL Dash
description: Interactive cricket analytics dashboard for IPL exploration, built to make match, team, player, and performance signals easier to scan through a public web interface.

title: AI Lead Discovery & CRM Improvement
description: Private consulting-demo workstream for agentic lead discovery, enrichment, scoring, account research, personalized outreach support, and CRM hygiene. Focused on improving pipeline quality and reducing manual prospecting work with auditable agent steps.
result: Structured prospecting into auditable stages for discovery, enrichment, scoring, research, outreach support, and CRM cleanup instead of an opaque end-to-end agent action.
why: Improves sales-research throughput while keeping evidence and human approval visible at each consequential step.
stack: Agentic workflows, enrichment, lead scoring, CRM automation, evidence logging, human approval
privacy: Capability described without exposing customer lists, prompts, workflows, or private code.

title: AI Governance Setup for Regulated Teams
description: Governance advisory and implementation work for HIPAA, finance, public-sector, and school contexts: AI readiness, policy mapping, risk registers, acceptable-use boundaries, vendor review, human oversight, evidence trails, and pilot evaluation.
result: Defined a reusable governance package spanning acceptable use, vendor review, risk ownership, human oversight, evidence trails, pilot criteria, and monitoring expectations.
why: Gives regulated teams an actionable path from policy language to controlled AI experimentation and accountable deployment.
stack: NIST AI RMF, policy mapping, model cards, vendor risk, impact assessment, monitoring design
privacy: Sector-level framing only; no client-specific controls, data, or confidential policies exposed.

title: Decision Intelligence
description: Decision-intelligence workstream connecting business questions, KPI definitions, scenario analysis, risk tradeoffs, and AI-assisted recommendation flows into decision-ready operating views for leaders and analysts.
result: Built a structured brief workflow that combines reference cases, strategic lenses, tradeoffs, and pre-mortems instead of returning a single unexamined recommendation.
why: Helps decision-makers pressure-test ambiguous choices while keeping uncertainty and competing interpretations visible.
stack: FastAPI, Next.js, pgvector, retrieval, LLM orchestration, scenario analysis

title: Operations Control Tower
problem: Operations teams need consistent table grain, KPI definitions, data-quality checks, and supplier-risk views before a dashboard can guide intervention.
contribution: Built the DuckDB star schema, validation checks, KPI layer, supplier-risk logic, and Looker Studio-ready executive views.
description: Operations-analytics control tower on 98k real Olist orders: SQL star schema, data-quality checks, supplier risk score, executive KPIs, and a Looker Studio-ready decision dashboard for logistics intervention planning.
result: Converted 98k real orders into a validated star schema, executive KPI layer, supplier-risk score, and Looker Studio-ready intervention views.
why: Moves operations reporting from descriptive totals toward a repeatable way to identify where logistics attention is needed.
stack: Python, DuckDB SQL, dimensional modeling, Great Expectations, Looker Studio

title: Healthcare Readmission Risk with Fairness & Calibration
problem: A readmission model can look strong in aggregate while remaining poorly calibrated or producing materially different errors across patient groups.
contribution: Designed the leakage-controlled cohort and evaluation, trained the models, analyzed calibration and subgroup errors, and wrote the model card.
description: Responsible-AI risk-modeling prototype on real UCI hospital data with leakage-controlled cohort design, calibration, subgroup fairness, SHAP, Fairlearn checks, and an honest model-card recommendation not to deploy as-is.
result: Produced a leakage-controlled benchmark with calibration, subgroup error analysis, SHAP, and a model-card recommendation not to deploy the model as-is.
why: Demonstrates that responsible healthcare ML includes knowing when performance and subgroup behavior do not justify deployment.
stack: Python, scikit-learn, XGBoost, Fairlearn, SHAP, MLflow

title: Tabular ML Benchmark: Human FE vs AutoML
problem: Teams often compare model families without isolating the effects of preprocessing, split design, calibration, runtime, and distribution shift.
contribution: Designed the config-driven benchmark, fixed the evaluation protocol, ran the cross-domain experiments, and interpreted performance, calibration, runtime, and shift robustness.
description: Config-driven benchmark across healthcare, operations, and labor-market datasets measuring AUC, calibration, runtime, and robustness under shift; shows preprocessing and validation design matter more than model shopping.
result: Across three domains and five pipelines, disciplined preprocessing improved AUC by up to 0.19 while hyperparameter tuning added almost no mean lift.
why: Shows why reproducible preprocessing, calibration, and validation under shift matter more than model shopping.
stack: Python, scikit-learn, MLflow, Evidently, config-driven experiments

title: SKM Football Analytics
problem: Isolated football event totals miss role, game state, and the process that makes decisive moments possible.
contribution: Developed and tested an original key-moment framework on StatsBomb open data, documented assumptions and role effects, and published reproducible analysis and writing.
description: Original football analytics research connecting event value, key moments, tactical context, and role-aware interpretation without presenting descriptive results as causal effects.
result: Produced a reproducible analysis and public methodology that makes the metric, its context, and its failure cases inspectable.
why: Relevant to sports analytics, media products, fan intelligence, player evaluation, and transfer analysis.
stack: Python, StatsBomb, pandas, probabilistic modeling, validation, Streamlit

title: Longitudinal Diabetes Risk Modeling with Neighborhood Deprivation
description: Built a longitudinal healthcare ML pipeline that converted sparse encounter records into patient-level cohorts and tested whether neighborhood deprivation improved prediction of uncontrolled HbA1c at 6-, 12-, and 24-month horizons.
result: The best observed ROC AUC was 0.612; matched ADI-ablation experiments showed no consistent lift, while follow-up coverage fell from 56.8% at 6 months to 40.8% at 24 months—making observation bias and target quality central findings.
why: A rigorous negative result showing cohort design, calibration, leakage control, and honest interpretation of social-risk variables—not a deployable clinical model.
stack: Python, pandas, scikit-learn, XGBoost, SHAP, longitudinal validation
privacy: Only aggregate outputs, figures, and deidentified methodology are public; no patient-level or institution-restricted data is shared.

title: Public-Sector AI Readiness & Efficiency Evaluation
description: Designed a four-stage decision framework for evaluating county AI opportunities before procurement or sensitive-data access, combining workflow baselines, public-data proxy studies, responsible-AI gates, resident-segment analysis, and controlled-pilot planning.
result: Turned open-ended AI adoption questions into ranked use cases, measurable pilot criteria, governance artifacts, and testable ROI hypotheses across document intake, policy retrieval, and service-request triage; simulated estimates are labeled as hypotheses, not production outcomes.
why: Shows the bridge from data science and AI evaluation to privacy, equity, accessibility, human review, and operational decision-making in a high-stakes public context.
stack: Python, scikit-learn, SHAP, public 311 data, document AI benchmarks, Power BI
privacy: Public evidence uses open or synthetic proxies; county and resident data, internal workflows, and confidential implementation details are not exposed.

title: Financial Document Intelligence (FinBizInfo)
description: Financial-statement OCR/RAG workflow for extracting ratios, risk flags, auditor remarks, and compliance gaps from noisy documents with citation-backed review and structured analytics.
role: AI Engineer / Financial Data Product Builder
result: Combined OCR, retrieval, structured extraction, risk flags, and citation-backed review in one live financial-analysis surface for noisy source documents.
why: Reduces manual document parsing while keeping analysts connected to the source evidence behind extracted financial claims.
stack: Python, OCR, RAG, LangChain, FastAPI, structured extraction, citation review

title: Freight Forwarding Finance BI
description: CargoWise-shaped Power BI and SQL analytics for freight forwarding finance: job margin variance, AR aging, DSO, revenue by mode/lane/branch, and reconciliation back to source reports.
result: Defined a reconciled KPI layer for job margin, AR aging, DSO, and revenue across mode, lane, and branch views using CargoWise-shaped reporting logic.
why: Gives freight-finance teams a consistent way to trace performance questions back to operational source reports.
stack: Power BI, DAX, SQL, CargoWise reporting, finance KPI design, reconciliation
privacy: Described at outcome level only; no client data, private repo, or internal dashboards exposed.

title: Data Science Career Audit
description: Source-backed labor-market analysis combining BLS/Stanford HAI/McKinsey/WEF signals with public job-posting NLP to explain how AI pressure changes the data-science role bundle.
result: Analyzed 728k public 2023 job postings plus a 2026 snapshot; GenAI/LLM language rose from roughly 0.1% of the 2023 corpus to about 27% of the later snapshot, reported descriptively rather than causally.
why: Separates durable data-science judgment from the job-title bundle and shows how hiring language is changing under AI pressure.
stack: Python, pandas, NLP, public job-posting data, descriptive statistics, reproducible reporting

title: SignalGraph
description: Credibility-adjusted signal layer for detecting fake-star and metric manipulation in open-source projects, framed for technical diligence and investor-style evidence review.
result: Reframed repository traction as an explainable combination of adoption, durability, builder quality, and manipulation risk instead of relying on raw star counts.
why: Provides investors and technical diligence teams with a more defensible signal for open-source credibility.
stack: FastAPI, PostgreSQL, graph signals, anomaly scoring, explainable risk, Recharts

title: Wax Seal Trust Provenance
description: Human-legible trust-provenance framework for digital artifacts, focused on visible tamper evidence, source lineage, and operational trust signals rather than opaque trust scores.
result: Delivered cryptographic seal envelopes, lifecycle state machines, CLI signing and verification, and reusable React components for visible provenance states.
why: Makes authenticity and tamper evidence understandable to people instead of hiding trust entirely inside backend cryptography.
stack: TypeScript, React, XState, Ed25519 signatures, CLI tooling, provenance schemas

title: Group of Death
description: Daily World Cup-themed draft-and-simulate game: pull player cards under constraints, build an XI, run a deterministic SKM match engine, and share the reveal card. Built as a React/Vite product with pure TypeScript game logic, Vercel functions, Upstash-backed daily seed/leaderboard support, and mobile-first interaction design.

title: Geo Visualizer for the Indian Subcontinent
description: Interactive historical flat-map visualization for culture, trade, migration, religion, political power, and ideas across the subcontinent with filters, routes, nodes, and explanatory context.
tweetText: Geo Visualizer for culture movement in subcontinent_p1 pic.twitter.com/IX9NMmKooI

title: Severance-Style Segmentation Interface
description: High-concept segmentation interface that turns customer, resident, or user segments into memorable identity modules with deliberate reveal, comparison views, and product-grade interaction design.
tweetText: Severance style data viz for user segmentation pic.twitter.com/HSUDU2DLIQ
